# 2026-01-17 20:04:53Z

<!-- VS Code Copilot IDE Session fcb3fbf8-69de-4d51-8d12-8d7ebdb6e924 (2026-01-17 20:04:53Z) -->

_**User (2026-01-17 20:04:53Z)**_

You are implementing an IDE + simulator + mod-runtime changes for the Stationeers mod
“Stationpedia Ascended”.

IMPORTANT:
• Do NOT claim anything is fixed unless you can point to exact code changes (files + key functions)
  AND provide concrete verification steps.
• If you cannot verify something (e.g., you cannot run the game), say so explicitly and explain
  how I should verify it in-game.
• Do NOT hallucinate fixes.

This work has TWO codebases:
A) Editor + Simulator (Electron / React tool)
B) Mod runtime (Stationeers mod code + JSON loading/rendering)

All changes below must be applied consistently across BOTH.

────────────────────────────────────────────
CORE CLARIFICATION ABOUT JSON (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• All edits MUST ultimately be written to the mod’s JSON files.
• The mod reads JSON directly.
• HOWEVER: the user must NEVER be required to manually edit JSON as their primary workflow.
• JSON is a persistence/output format, not the authoring interface.
• All user edits occur via GUI → internal AST → serialized JSON.

────────────────────────────────────────────
CRITICAL SCHEMA REFACTOR: OPERATIONAL DETAILS
────────────────────────────────────────────

This is a schema-level refactor, not a UI tweak.

CURRENT PROBLEMS:
• “Flat Structure” breaks formatting and nested layout.
• Without Flat Structure, everything is forcibly wrapped in “Operational Details”.
• “Operational Details” is hard-coded, cannot be renamed, and behaves inconsistently.
• Nested boxes look broken and do not match in-game behavior.

THE “FLAT STRUCTURE” CONCEPT MUST BE REMOVED ENTIRELY.

NEW AUTHORITATIVE MODEL:
• “Operational Details” is NOT a special wrapper.
• It is simply the DEFAULT category created when a page is initialized.
• It behaves exactly like any other category.
• It can be renamed, deleted, reordered, or replaced.

This model applies universally:
• Devices
• Guides
• Game Mechanics
• Any Stationpedia page

────────────────────────────────────────────
NEW UNIFIED PAGE STRUCTURE (REQUIRED)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Every Stationpedia page must follow this structure:

1) Image (optional, editable)
   • Uses game PNG assets from the AssetRipper folder
   • Editable in the editor
   • Rendered in simulator and in-game using the same rules as existing pages

2) Title
   • Single authoritative title
   • Editable
   • No duplicates anywhere in editor or simulator

3) Page Description
   • Supports prepend / append description blocks (as currently supported)
   • Rich text editable

4) Categories (one or more)
   • Categories are first-class objects
   • “Operational Details” is the default initial category only
   • Categories are renameable, reorderable, addable, and deletable

────────────────────────────────────────────
CATEGORY CONTENT MODEL (CRITICAL)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Inside each category, only the following primitives are allowed:

A) Nested Section
   • Behaves like current Operational Details nesting
   • Collapsible / expandable
   • Can contain:
     – Paragraph text
     – Media blocks
     – Other nested sections

B) Inline Header Block
   • Matches vanilla guides behavior
   • Header text (H1/H2/H3)
   • Followed by paragraph blocks directly underneath
   • NOT collapsible
   • Can exist between nested sections

There is NO flat mode.

All layout is composed using these primitives.

────────────────────────────────────────────
EDITOR + SIMULATOR REQUIREMENTS
────────────────────────────────────────────

EDITOR:
• Remove “Flat Structure” button entirely.
• Add:
  – Add Category
  – Add Nested Section
  – Add Inline Header
• Word-processor-style rich text editing.
• Drag-and-drop reordering of:
  – Categories
  – Nested sections
  – Inline headers
• Resizable, dockable, pop-out panels.

SIMULATOR:
• Separate window.
• Renders pages using deterministic HTML/CSS Stationpedia Renderer.
• Fully interactive:
  – Navigation (home/back/forward/search)
  – Mode toggle (Vanilla hides Ascended overrides; Ascended shows full mod content)
• Live-updating on edits (debounced).
• Uses game assets only (no invented UI styling).

────────────────────────────────────────────
MEDIA SUPPORT (REQUIRED)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Add support for:
  – YouTube video link blocks
  – Image blocks
• Formatting MUST exactly match the existing Solid Fuel Generator device page.
  That page is the canonical reference.
• Editor must allow adding/editing these blocks.
• Simulator must preview them correctly.

────────────────────────────────────────────
DEVICE PAGE COMPLETENESS (MANDATORY)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Currently only “Logic Types” are shown. This is incorrect.

• The editor and simulator MUST render and allow editing of ALL device page categories
  present in JSON (slots, connections, modes, IO, power, atmos, network, recipes, etc.).
• Rendering must be data-driven, not hard-coded.
• Known categories → custom UI.
• Unknown categories → generic renderer + inspector editor.
• NO category present in JSON may be hidden.

────────────────────────────────────────────
GLOBAL TOOLTIP SYSTEM
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Rename “Tooltips” button to “Global Tooltips”.
• Clicking a global tooltip MUST open an editor.
• Edits must persist and reflect in simulator and in-game.
• Device-specific tooltips remain editable on device pages.
• Global Tooltips screen is only for shared tooltips.

────────────────────────────────────────────
COMPLETE DATA COVERAGE (LOSSLESS)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• 100% of Stationpedia JSON data must be preserved.
• Unknown or unmodeled fields must round-trip unchanged.
• Provide an “All Fields / Inspector” panel:
  – Shows full JSON object
  – Auto-generated editable controls for unknown fields
  – Optional raw JSON editor (advanced fallback only)

────────────────────────────────────────────
STATIONPEDIA RENDERER VISUAL FIDELITY
────────────────────────────────────────────

• There are NO cyan links in Stationeers. Remove all cyan link styling.
• Use the SAME assets the game uses (from AssetRipper).
• Do not invent UI styling.
• Add a theme mapping file (e.g., stationpediaTheme.json) mapping UI elements → asset paths.
• Add a “Theme Debug” mode showing which asset is used for each UI element.

────────────────────────────────────────────
MOD RUNTIME FIXES (IN-GAME)
────────────────────────────────────────────

1) Guide button color must match vanilla guides (light blue).
   • Find actual color used by the game. Do not guess.

2) “Vanilla Guides” font must match “Guides” font.

3) Tooltip bug when game is paused:
   • Tooltips must still appear while paused.

4) “Stationeers Survival Manual” button:
   • Must appear ONLY on its own page.
   • Must NOT appear as a button on the Guides page.

────────────────────────────────────────────
MIGRATION REQUIREMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Existing content using the old Operational Details wrapper must be migrated to:
  – A normal category named “Operational Details”
  – With all nested content preserved
• No duplicate titles.
• No broken nesting.
• No data loss.

────────────────────────────────────────────
VERIFICATION / NO-HALLUCINATION REQUIREMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────

For EACH implemented item:
1) List files changed and key functions/classes.
2) Describe how it was broken before.
3) Describe how it works now.
4) Provide steps to verify in:
   – Editor
   – Simulator
   – In-game (if applicable)

If something cannot be verified locally, say so explicitly.

Do not proceed with partial fixes.
Implement these changes consistently across editor, simulator, and mod runtime.

   - Tooltip pause bug location

- All JSON loading/saving code
- Schema definitions
- CSS/styling files
- C# rendering code in the mod

Return structured findings with file paths, key functions/classes, and current implementation details. Do NOT write plans, only research and return findings.
